source_title: Code blocks lose formatting when converting from HTML to markdown
signal_summary: [Verified] Crawl4ai users reported that HTML pre/code blocks were flattened, removing line breaks and indentation in Markdown output.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users need readable and structurally faithful Markdown because broken formatting damages downstream extraction and human review.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should preserve structural fidelity for code-like and layout-sensitive content and surface when fidelity is degraded.

source_title: [Bug]: result formats markdown and cleaned_html will include damaged html tables
signal_summary: [Verified] Crawl4ai users reported table corruption in Markdown and cleaned HTML because empty columns were removed.
why_users_care: [Verified] Broken tables make extracted data unreliable for LLM parsing, spreadsheet transfer, and operator trust.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should treat tables as a high-risk fidelity area and provide explicit quality signals for structured outputs.

source_title: Academic PDF conversion outputs non-standard Markdown with broken formatting
signal_summary: [Verified] MarkItDown users reported broken Markdown formatting on academic PDF conversion.
why_users_care: [Verified] Long-form documents lose value if headings, citations, lists, and layout collapse during conversion.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should avoid broad PDF-to-Markdown confidence claims without document-class testing and fallback messaging.

source_title: Unable to crawl heavy Javascript based website
signal_summary: [Verified] Jina Reader users reported failure on JS-heavy websites.
why_users_care: [Verified] Real customer targets increasingly depend on client-side rendering, hydration, and delayed content population.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should make render strategy and wait behavior visible so users can understand SPA coverage boundaries.

source_title: [Self-Host] Firecrawl fails on sites with strong anti-bot measures where Browserless.io succeeds on the same host
signal_summary: [Verified] Firecrawl users reported failure on strong anti-bot sites in self-hosted mode even on infrastructure where Browserless succeeded.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users care because anti-bot failures can invalidate the tool for high-value targets even when network access and browser mode exist.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should expose anti-bot failure classification and avoid presenting browser rendering as an automatic bypass.

signal_summary: [Verified] A Puppeteer Extra user reported Cloudflare blocking access even when simply connecting to the browser.
why_users_care: [Verified] Detection risk can appear before page interaction, which means browser automation alone is not a reliable anti-bot answer.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should communicate that browser-based extraction still has fingerprinting limits and may need fallback or explicit non-support messaging.

source_title: We built authenticated scraping into our API, store your cookies once and scrape logged-in pages on every request
signal_summary: [Verified] The post framed reusable saved-cookie sessions as the product abstraction users want for logged-in scraping.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users care because the highest-value pages are often behind authentication and repeated cookie injection is operationally clumsy.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] If Oleriq moves into authenticated extraction, session objects or session IDs are likely a clearer user model than raw cookie blobs.

source_title: Phone-native alternative to login scraping using real sessions
signal_summary: [Verified] The thread described login scraping as fragile because of 2FA, modern bot detection, and session reuse complexity.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users care because public-page extraction misses the data they actually need from dashboards, portals, and account views.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should assume authenticated extraction needs stable session reuse and explicit session-health checks rather than one-off login automation.

source_title: [Bug]: Persistent Context (userDataDir) Fails Cookie Read & Corrupts Profile
signal_summary: [Verified] A Playwright user reported persistent browser context problems including cookie-read failure and corrupted profile behavior.
why_users_care: [Verified] Session reuse is hard if the underlying browser-state primitive behaves differently across modes or damages the profile.
recency_note: [Verified] GitHub issue was active in 2026 and crawled last week; the issue page shows it remains a recent problem.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should avoid assuming browser profile persistence is a solved foundation for authenticated workflows.

source_title: The flowchart in Word document containing Visio or the UML in Visio cannot be converted to MD file correctly
signal_summary: [Verified] MarkItDown users reported incorrect Markdown conversion for Word documents containing Visio or UML flowcharts.
why_users_care: [Verified] Office extraction demand includes embedded diagrams and mixed-content documents, not just body text.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should scope Office support by document subtype and embedded-content behavior, not just file extension.

source_title: [BUG] docling-tools models download easyocr only downloads English/Latin models, causing failures for non-English documents
signal_summary: [Verified] Docling users reported non-English OCR failure in offline mode because only English and Latin models were downloaded.
why_users_care: [Verified] Multilingual document support affects actual extraction coverage, deployment packaging, and runtime cost.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should treat multilingual OCR coverage and artifact packaging as explicit product constraints rather than hidden defaults.

signal_summary: [Verified] Docling users reported certain PDFs hanging during conversion.
why_users_care: [Verified] A stalled document job is worse than a clean failure because it blocks batch throughput and obscures the true state of work.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should enforce timeouts and explicit stuck-job reporting on document pipelines.

source_title: [Bug] No Data is Returned After Crawling (formats-JSON)
signal_summary: [Verified] Firecrawl users reported jobs marked completed and successful while returning an empty data array.
why_users_care: [Verified] False-success states break automation trust because the control plane says success while the payload says failure.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should distinguish transport completion, render completion, and data success in batch status reporting.

source_title: The request queue scans all 450k (99.999% of which are done towards the end) requests for each iteration
signal_summary: [Verified] Crawlee users reported extreme queue inefficiency near job completion because the queue kept scanning almost all completed requests.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users care because queue inefficiency drives runtime, infrastructure cost, and uncertainty on large recurring jobs.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] If Oleriq adds recurring queues, it should expose queue efficiency and retry behavior instead of hiding them behind a generic progress state.

source_title: [Feat] Remove cookie banner/pop up from screenshots
signal_summary: [Verified] Firecrawl users asked for screenshot cleanup because cookie banners and privacy popups block meaningful content.
why_users_care: [Verified] Visual verification is less useful if the screenshot captures overlays rather than the content users intended to inspect.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq can improve trust by treating screenshot cleanliness as part of extraction quality, not as a cosmetic extra.

source_title: Extremely high token usage on s.jina.ai — spikes up to 1.9M tokens per single request
signal_summary: [Verified] Jina Reader users reported token spikes up to 1.9 million tokens for a single request.
why_users_care: [Verified] Output-size unpredictability creates direct cost risk and makes agent loops harder to budget or bound.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should expose output-size or token-risk indicators when extraction can balloon unexpectedly.

source_title: Bug: Initial actions not saved to history and incomplete serialization breaking replay functionality
signal_summary: [Verified] Browser Use users reported replay failure because initial actions were not stored and history serialization was incomplete.
why_users_care: [Verified] Users need automation artifacts that can be replayed and debugged deterministically after exploratory runs.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] If Oleriq captures selectors or actions, it should store enough state for full replay instead of just partial execution traces.

signal_summary: [Verified] Playwright users requested configurable selector-building attributes because real teams use custom test IDs and locator conventions.
why_users_care: [Verified] Recorder output becomes brittle if it does not match the selector strategy actually used in production apps.
recency_note: [Verified] GitHub issue opened September 19, 2021 and still remains open as a feedback signal.
clearpage_implication: [Inference] Oleriq should assume deterministic handoff requires adaptable selector strategies, not one default locator heuristic.
